item.content:encoded: Noice

item.title: Nice
item.content:encoded:
Nice

1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: nice

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nice
BUT 420 +1s
IS DARE I SAY NICER

item.title: nice
item.content:encoded: nice

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ha

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I swear to god if someone makes A "69" joke… (also good Article).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
nice.

But in all reality, this SCP is interesting, but mostly I see it as sad.
Imagine that all that all the sudden you "survive" an impossible feat, not realizing that it's not really you. Your ideas, memories, knowledge, isn't really yours.
Top that with being locked up in a strange facility and then learn you're not you. You're something else,and you don't know it, you don't feel it, you don't remember it. It's sounds like a sad existence.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank God I'm not the only one who thought that

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Suicidal body jumper? I like

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: nice

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: this one is both terrifying and… seemingly kind of nice. While it Does impersonate you, it also works to ensure you have no dying regrets. In an earlier post someone was curious as to what would happen if Bright died near it, and i'm curious myself. Would it take on Bright's personality, or the [former] personality of his host?

item.title: Use of Class D
item.content:encoded:
I know the use of Class D personnel as an impersonation target probably won't end very well if it breaches containment, but…

What about the use of Class D personnel with no surviving family members, outstanding debts, personal obligations, and any other considerations that SCP-069 might be compelled to resolve? What about setting up a screening process to select a single D-class who match the following in the event that SCP-069 dies? Out of hundreds, if not thousands, of individuals with virtually nothing to lose, this shouldn't be too much of a problem.

Optimally, this person in question should be fairly young, be in good health, and is generally compliant with the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow this one is truly horrifying

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of those that just makes me sad, in a happy way. It's one of those where you read it, and then you read the title, and then you read a specific sentence, and then the point of the article hits you right in the feels at that exact moment and you have the 'ah-ha' moment. I love articles like this. I encourage anyone hanging out in Recent Posts to come on over, if you haven't already.

item.title: I like him/her
item.content:encoded: What a nice SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was thinking more along the lines of let the Agent kill himself, but have another Agent ready. Like brief him of what he would be doing and getting himself into, maybe record it so that if he doesn't remember it after reanimating they have proof.

I realize your comment is years old but figured I'd still put in my two cents.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, why have those plans been suspended?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just reread 069, and wasn't sure how to vote. Then I read this comment. I read Final Regrets before, but didn't connect it to 069. Very nice, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Relevant to this topic: Final Regrets
Congrats!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm quite curious as to what would happen if Dr. Bright died near it…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, am I ever a sucker for these tear-jerker tragedies. The tone is perfectly executed I must say. Have a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Cool concept. Other than the "returned" vs. "retained" error, this is one of the best IMO.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How did I miss upvoting this one? Front page feature definitely deserved.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because a Foundation Agent, even suicidal one, is easier to contain, and less dangerous to others if it escapes, than a D-class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a question…why not allow it to take on the appearance of a random D-class personnel, so that it can relinquish the agent? It seems that the fact that it practically IS this Agent, at least as far as it knows, is why it wants to commit suicide. That would probably stop the suicide problem and they wouldn't have to worry about tracking it down, since it takes on whoever's closest (and dead.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Granted, given the nature of 069, a murder spree would be somewhat difficult…"

He could always fatally injure them in such a way that he'll be out of range before they're dead. But then again, it says he doesn't know of his ability, so he wouldn't know to do that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one, it's a nice take on the trope of characters turning to dust on death.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For a second there, I thought it was one of Fringe's Shapeshifters.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Plus the danger of losing track of it if it inadvertently dies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems to me that the most likely reason SCP-069 is being utilized tentatively at best is that the Foundation doesn't want to have to rely on a known SCP. Benevolent or not, SCP-069 is both an unknown quantity and an unpredictable one. So while it might be useful to test some of its abilities in a narrow sense, it's definitely not something you want to risk losing, if only because it'd be hard to find again and you don't know if you can count on it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh, it's a recurring theme in fiction. Copies don't take well to being called out as copies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno. For a mass murderer, finishing up that killing spree might help give him a nice sense of closure. Besides, getting things in order is the only change noted in their behavior. Once they've done so, there's no reason why they wouldn't return to other activities. Granted, given the nature of 069, a murder spree would be somewhat difficult…

And as for your second point, that may very well have been the individual response of the agent 069 was impersonating. There's no reason to think that it would respond that way every single time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Except two things:
1) It presents the same motivation regardless of its target. Becoming a recently-executed mass-murderer wouldn't give it a sense of "YAY ALIVE, KILL MORE PEOPLEZ VWEEHEEHEE!"
2) Apparently, it can be countered by being told family knows the person's dead.

Hate to be a voice of dissent, but it's starting to feel a bit too useful…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Even aside from the fact that the Foundation contains SCPs regardless of how beneficial they are in the wild, SCP-069's impersonation is involuntary. Whenever it is alone with any recently dead body, it will turn into that person, regardless of whether it's someone who actually deserves a "second chance".

Also, if SCP-069 dies for any reason, it will randomly jump away to another dead body up to several hundred miles away, making tracking difficult at best.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Really cool idea. I think the indistinguishability rings a little off with me: if the SCP is this identical to the person it impersonates, why does it need to be contained? ie, why not just track the Agent it is impersonating and let it go about its business? As written presently it appears there is little doubt that 069 is safe, and in fact seems kinda benevolent.

Two ideas: the more SCPish would be to write in some nagging doubts, someone's suspicion that 069 has differences from the original that may be less friendly than it seems. Alternately, maybe more in line with the intent of the article, perhaps at some point 069 has taken the personality of a violent criminal, resurrecting him/her/it after he/she/it was finally taken down in a firefight. This could make 069 worth containing just so that the risk of this or similar misuse is eliminated.

A final thought. If confirmed safe, 069 could be fantastically useful on dangerous exploration missions. It basically gives the personnel on the mission a "free life"; if the mission operative is killed in action 069 can adopt his identity and possibly make it back to report as much as possible on what happened. Not that you need to write anything about it being used this way but it could be a neat thought to have 'pending approval' or something.

Whew. That was more than I meant to write, sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Indeed. Fixing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it as well, just one minor thing:

"SCP-069 also returns no knowledge of its abilities or former impersonations."

Do you mean "retains" instead of "returns"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it. I think you have broken the 069 curse.
